support_note: Refrigerator variant is a canonical example of new-product line design — the Egyptian case (73 stations) shows what a new refrigerator line model looks like.
Refrigerator variant explicitly identifies Station 7 as the bottleneck, making it the primary reference for the bottleneck-mitigation scenario.
Washing machine variant covers new-product station allocation for 100+ component assemblies.
Midea washing machine case reports a 20% line balance improvement, reaching >90% balance rate — canonical bottleneck-mitigation outcome.
High-end washing machine variant with flexibility improvements explicitly addresses mixed-model sequencing (multiple SKUs on a shared line).
Air conditioner variant based on PMC paper shows 29-station line design with 68-to-85% balance improvement.
Air conditioner variant explicitly reduces bottleneck from 96.67 to 74.6 seconds.
PMC air conditioner paper documents 10-hour single-shift baseline, enabling scenario comparisons against multi-shift configurations, though the paper itself does not test shift variations directly.
Generic teaching variant supports Scenario 1 at a pedagogical level, using simplified parameter sets rather than domain-specific case study data.
